tom loop size 0.7, same recipe as pad5 with size-graded values

cymbal loop (NOT in crafted -> disabled in final preset)

kits.weaknesses: Crafted set is only 13 pads (kick/snare/sidestick/3 hats/6 toms/1 cymbal); pads 15-31 are configured identically in loops then DISABLED, so the kit is functionally small. Toms are 6 near-identical Membrane/Mallet voices differing only by size + a tiny b1 ramp (0.30+0.02*i) and secondary size ramp — exactly the audit §4 sameness complaint (one timbre stamped with a size sweep). The 7 cymbal slots are byte-identical NoiseBody loops. modeStretch left at default 0.333 (so the corrected B_max=0.01 metallic axis is unused on toms/cymbals), decaySkew default 0.5 everywhere (the now-working per-mode tilt unused), modeInject 0 everywhere (the 1/k fix unused), pan 0.5 everywhere (M-9 stereo unused). Snare leans heavily on click(0.92)+noise(0.82) over body — the H-2 'noisy thwack masks body' pattern, though it now adds real shell coupling.

kits.weaknesses: Best-developed Acoustic kit (macros + couplingAmount actually used, two distinct snare flavours, brush snare uses friction sweep + morph rather than noise crack). Still the 6-tom row is a size/material/pitch sweep over ONE recipe (audit §4 #3). modeStretch unused on toms/membranes (default 0.333), decaySkew default on all drums, modeInject 0 throughout, pan 0.5 throughout. Ride/crash duplicate the cymbal-as-NoiseBody/Bell pattern. Brush snare's reliance on noise layer is by design (no body crack) so it's a defensible exception, not the H-2 problem.

kits.weaknesses: Uses kit-level globals (globalCoupling 0.30, snareBuzz 0.35, tomResonance 0.45, maxPoly 12) — more dialed than acousticKit. Snare/kick are well-voiced with coupling. Same 6-tom sweep-of-one-recipe structure. modeStretch default everywhere, decaySkew default everywhere, modeInject 0 everywhere, pan 0.5 everywhere (audit §4 #4/#7 axes unused). Crash/ride/splash all derive from a single NoiseBody crash via clone+tweak. Strongly leans on click(0.85-0.95)+noise on kick/snare/rim.

Snare/kick/tom recipes are structurally the SAME as the other 3 acoustic kits (Membrane+Mallet/NoiseBurst, click+noise+coupling), so cross-kit sameness within the Acoustic category is high. modeStretch only on wood blocks; decaySkew only on pad1 (0.30); modeInject 0 throughout; pan 0.5 throughout.

kits.weaknesses: Most varied Acoustic kit by body palette (Membrane timpani, Bell triangle/gong/crotales, String tubular bell, NoiseBody cymbals) and uses morph + decaySkew + modeStretch on several metallic pads. Headroom comments reveal gain-staging tuning predates N-1 fix (level/airLoading/secondary cut to avoid the OLD clip) — these conservative cuts should be REVISITED now that the body norm is measured-strike-bounded. modeInject 0 throughout; pan 0.5 throughout; decaySkew still default on the 5 timpani toms (the per-mode tilt fix unused there). Bell/gong voiced against the OLD bell strike shape — must be re-voiced per audit Phase 2 step 7.

kits.weaknesses: 808 toms have the best pitch-env motion in the library (long glide times). Snares use a metallic secondary body (good differentiation). But airLoading/coupling/scatter deliberately ZEROED ('clean modal pitches') means most physics axes are off — the kit relies on pitchEnv + secondary body + noise/click only. modeStretch default everywhere (the metallic shimmer body could exploit the new B_max), decaySkew default everywhere, modeInject 0 throughout, pan 0.5 throughout. 19 FM-bell perc slots configured identically then disabled (only pad1 used).

kits.weaknesses: Snare has good dual-layer design (metallic secondary 0.90 + long noise tail). Toms are 6 Impulse/Membrane with a pitch sweep — structurally the same as the 909/electronic tom rows in the sibling kits, differing only in exciter (Impulse vs Mallet). All physics axes zeroed (airLoading/scatter 0), modeStretch default, decaySkew default, modeInject 0, pan 0.5 — same Electronic-category profile as electronicKit. Clap is the only pad using noiseResonance for a formant. Only 14 crafted; pads 1,16-31 default+disabled.

kits.weaknesses: Retro character comes ONLY from darker noise (cutoff 0.65, color 0.50) and FMImpulse on kick/cowbell/clave — a thin distinction from 909. Snare lacks the secondary metallic body the 808/909 snares have, so it is structurally a simpler version of the same Membrane+NoiseBurst recipe. All physics axes off (airLoading/scatter 0), modeStretch default, decaySkew default, modeInject 0, pan 0.5. Toms again a single Mallet/Membrane recipe size-swept. 14 crafted, 15-31 disabled.

kits.weaknesses: Most experimental Electronic kit: Feedback/FMImpulse/Friction exciters, Plate/Bell/String bodies, heavy nonlinearCoupling (0.30-0.55) and modeStretch use, morph on snare/drone, globalCoupling 0.65 + tomResonance 0.55. CRITICAL re-voicing flag: every nonlinearCoupling and Drive value here was set against the OLD broken stages (M-2 un-compensated drive makeup, M-3/M-4 transient-only/full-signal recipSqrt) — per audit Phase 4 these MUST be re-voiced against the redesigned env-level waveshaping. modeInject still 0 throughout (the 1/k fix unused); decaySkew used but not heavily; pan 0.5 throughout. Plate toms voiced against OLD SSSS ratios — re-voice per Phase 2.

Snare/kick recipes still the same Membrane base as other Electronic kits. Only 14 crafted (note: tom row skips pad7, used as a 3rd closed hat). Physics axes off (airLoading 0/0.10, scatter low), modeStretch default, decaySkew only on kick (0.45), modeInject 0 throughout, pan 0.5 throughout. stealingPolicy 2, maxPoly 16.

kits.weaknesses: Good use of strikePosition (the only kit family that varies it per-pad: bass 0.45-0.50 vs slap 0.08-0.15) — exploits the audit-confirmed working Membrane strike weighting. BUT strikePosition is dead/wrong on the Plate cajon/woodblock pads (audit §B: Plate strike shape was wrong, now fixed but these were voiced before). Latent bug: crafted list includes pad 14 which is never configured -> a default Membrane voice sounds. modeStretch only on cajon/woodblock; decaySkew only on frame drum; modeInject 0 throughout; pan 0.5 throughout; tension low. Conga/bongo/djembe are all the same Impulse/Membrane recipe distinguished by size + pitchEnv + strikePos.

kits.weaknesses: Heavily Bell-based (clave/cowbell/agogo/triangle all Bell+FM/Impulse) — these were voiced against the OLD bell strike shape (audit §B, now fixed), so the partial balance is wrong and must be re-voiced. The clave/cowbell/agogo pairs are clone+detune of one Bell recipe. modeStretch only on bells (0.50-0.55, all near default); decaySkew only on guiro; modeInject 0 throughout; pan 0.5 throughout (a pan-spread would greatly help a perc kit). Tambourine's metallic secondary (0.85) is a nice touch. Shaker/maracas/cabasa are 3 near-identical NoiseBody noise patches.

But only 10 crafted pads, and 5 of them (1,6,7,8) are clones of 0/2/3. modeStretch only on tanpura; modeInject 0 throughout; pan 0.5 throughout. Dayan/Tha/Na all the same ~0.42-size Membrane differing by exciter/decaySkew/morph. The kit is small and would benefit from filling the empty 22 slots. Strong candidate to KEEP its tension/decaySkew character while re-voicing the String tanpura against any String changes.

Almost entirely Bell+Mallet (kalimba 8, crotales 2, tingsha, temple bell, bell tree) plus String mbira — heavily exposed to the OLD bell strike-shape bug (audit §B, now fixed) so EVERY metallic pad needs re-voicing. The 8 kalimba pads are one Bell recipe with a material/size/fmRatio ramp (audit §4 #4 sameness). modeStretch present but all near default 0.40-0.55 (the new B_max axis barely used). modeInject 0 throughout despite being a metallic kit that would benefit from inharmonic injection. decaySkew used well (0.55-0.85). pan 0.5 throughout.

Frame/bodhran/dholak are the same Impulse/Membrane recipe differing by size + airLoading + pitchEnv (overlaps heavily with handDrumsKit - cross-kit Percussive sameness). 3 tabla-style fillers are clone+detune of one recipe. modeStretch only on cajon pads; modeInject 0 throughout; decaySkew only on cajon-snare/dholak; pan 0.5 throughout. Riq's metallic secondary (0.85) is a highlight.

kits.weaknesses: The ONLY kit that uses ALL 32 pads (no crafted gating) and the ONLY kit using modeInjectAmount (0.2 on the FX cycle). Most diverse body/exciter palette. BUT the 20-pad FX cycle is fully algorithmic (i-indexed ramps over body cycle), so adjacent FX pads differ only by small parametric increments - varied but not individually crafted. CRITICAL re-voice flag: Feedback snare + all nonlinearCoupling/Drive values voiced against OLD M-2/M-3/M-4 stages; Plate toms + Shell/Bell FX pads voiced against OLD SSSS/bell/shell shapes (Phase 2). modeStretch used (0.2-0.5). decaySkew default on most. pan 0.5 throughout. globalCoupling not set (default 0).

kits.name: Glass Bell Garden

pads.notableValues: all-Bell loop (0-15): exciter i%4==3 Friction, i%3==0 Mallet, else FMImpulse; material 0.40+i/15*0.55, size 0.10+(15-i)/15*0.60, decay 0.55+(i%6)*0.07, fmRatio 0.30+, nonlinearCoupling (i%4)*0.10, decaySkew 0.55+(i%4)*0.08, modeScatter 0.30+, morph on i%5==0, secondaryMaterial 0.85, noise/click graded; couplingAmount 0.55+

kits.weaknesses: Entirely ONE body (Bell) for all 16 crafted pads, voiced via i-indexed parametric ramps. This is the PUREST example of audit §4 #4 sameness: the bell partial topology is fixed, so size/material/fmRatio/decaySkew only scale and tilt the SAME skeleton across all 16 pads. ALL pads voiced against the OLD bell strike shape (audit §B) - the entire kit must be re-voiced. modeInject 0 throughout (a bell garden is exactly where 1/k inharmonic injection would add variety - missed). modeStretch present but capped low (0.30-0.72). pan 0.5 throughout (a bell garden screams for stereo spread). globalCoupling 0.65, couplingAmount used.

kits.name: Drone and Sustain

pads.notableValues: loop 0-7: body even=String odd=Membrane; material 0.35->0.85, size 0.40+, decay 0.92 (all long), frictionPressure 0.35+, nonlinearCoupling 0.40+, decaySkew 0.85, filter LP cutoff 0.45 res 0.35 with full ADSR (atk 0.20 sus 0.55 rel 0.65), coupling 0.18 secondary, tension 0.25+, noise 0.18+, outputBus i%2; couplingAmount 0.85

loop 8-13: body even=Plate odd=Shell; material 0.40+, size 0.45+, decay 0.92, feedback 0.40+, nonlinearCoupling 0.55, decaySkew 0.78, filter BP cutoff 0.55 res 0.45, modeScatter 0.40, airLoading 0.20, coupling 0.30, tension 0.45, noise 0.30, outputBus 1; couplingAmount 0.85

kits.weaknesses: The only sustain/drone kit - all decay 0.92, heavy nonlinearCoupling (0.40-0.55), filter ADSR sustain used (the only kit with sustained filter envelopes), high globalCoupling 0.85 + tomResonance 0.65. Two algorithmic halves (8 Friction strings, 6 Feedback plates/shells), each an i-indexed parametric ramp - varied but not individually crafted. CRITICAL re-voice: nonlinearCoupling everywhere voiced against OLD M-3/M-4 (now sustained env-level waveshaping - drones will sound very different); Feedback path now has the M-6 secondary shell + tension restored (was dropped) so 8-13 will change; Plate/Shell shapes corrected (Phase 2). modeInject 0 throughout; pan 0.5 (uses outputBus i%2 for some L/R but no equal-power pan). decaySkew heavy (0.78-0.85).

kits.name: Chaos Engine

pads.notableValues: loop 0-13: exciter i%3 cycle, body i%6 cycle; material/size fmod-ramped, decay 0.40+, feedback 0.40+, frictionPressure 0.30+, modeStretch 0.40+(i%5)*0.12, nonlinearCoupling 0.65+(i%3)*0.10 (HIGHEST in lib), decaySkew 0.40+, morph on i%2==0, filter cycle LP/HP/BP res 0.55+, drive 0.30+, fold 0.20+(i%3)*0.18, scatter 0.55+, coupling 0.40+ secondary ON, tension 0.78+(i%4)*0.05 (HIGHEST), full noise+click, chokeGroup i<4?0:(i%2)+1, outputBus i%4, pitchEnv 180+/40+, macros all spread; couplingAmount 0.78

kits.weaknesses: Maxes out nearly every knob (nonlinearCoupling 0.65-0.85, tension 0.78-0.93, fold, drive, all bodies/exciters cycled, globalCoupling 0.92, snareBuzz 0.55, tomResonance 0.78, stealingPolicy 2). Despite that, it is fully algorithmic over 14 pads - no individually-crafted voices. CRITICAL re-voice: this kit leans HARDEST on the redesigned/fixed stages (nonlinearCoupling now env-level waveshaping, Drive makeup, Feedback secondary shell restored, all body shapes corrected, tension recalibrated ~2 st) so it will sound DRAMATICALLY different post-fix and is the highest-priority re-voice. modeInject still 0 throughout (ironic for a 'chaos' kit). pan 0.5 (uses outputBus i%4). Likely now near or over the rail given max-everything values predate N-1.

kits.name: Ghost Bones

pads.notableValues: loop 0-13: body even=Bell odd=String; exciter i%5==4 Friction else i%3 cycle Mallet/FMImpulse/Impulse; material 0.30+, size 0.20+(13-i)/13*0.55, decay 0.85-, modeStretch 0.65+(i%4)*0.08 (HIGH stretch), nonlinearCoupling 0.20+, decaySkew 0.78+ (HIGH), morph on i%3!=0, filter HP cutoff 0.18+ res 0.30, scatter 0.40+, coupling i%2 secondary, tension 0.30+, noise low, click low, outputBus i%4==3?1:0; couplingAmount 0.85; macroComplexity 0.85

kits.weaknesses: Skeletal/eerie character from high modeStretch (0.65-0.89 - the most aggressive use of the now-widened B_max axis), high decaySkew (0.78+), HP filter, long decays. Fully algorithmic over 14 Bell/String pads. CRITICAL re-voice: Bell pads against OLD bell shape (Phase 2); modeStretch values were set against the OLD off-by-one + B_max=0.001 (now corrected to (k+1)/B_max=0.01) so the stretch is now FAR more extreme than intended - this kit will sound much more inharmonic and MUST be re-checked. modeInject 0 throughout. pan 0.5 (uses outputBus). globalCoupling 0.78. This kit's high-stretch reliance makes it the SECOND-highest re-voice priority after chaosEngine.

padStructFieldMap:
Generator Pad-struct field -> controller param name -> PadParamOffset enum -> physical denormalization (from voice_pool.cpp::applyPadConfigToSlot + drum_voice.h). All Pad-struct values are NORMALIZED [0,1] on the wire.

SELECTORS (int, not normalized):
- exciterType -> "Exciter Type" (kPadExciterType=0). 0=Impulse,1=Mallet,2=NoiseBurst,3=Friction,4=FMImpulse,5=Feedback.
- bodyModel -> "Body Model" (kPadBodyModel=1). 0=Membrane,1=Plate,2=Shell,3=String,4=Bell,5=NoiseBody.

CORE:
- material -> "Material" (kPadMaterial=2, default 0.5). Body-specific brightness/stiffness; feeds mapper.
- size -> "Size" (kPadSize=3, default 0.5). Drives fundamental: f0 ~= 500*0.1^size Hz (larger size -> lower f0; e.g. 0.5->~158Hz, 0.9->~31Hz).
- decay -> "Decay" (kPadDecay=4, default 0.3). Base t60; combines with bodyDampingB1 override.
- strikePosition -> "Strike Position" (kPadStrikePosition=5, default 0.3). Mode-shape weighting; norm [0,1] across the body (radius/azimuth depending on body).
- level -> "Level" (kPadLevel=6, default 0.8). True linear voice attenuation (post-N-1 gain-staging).

TONE SHAPER:
- tsFilterType -> "Filter Type" (kPadTSFilterType=7). clamp(int(v*3),0,2): 0=LP,1=HP,2=BP (the FilterType:: namespace uses LP=0.0,HP=0.5,BP=1.0).
- tsFilterCutoff -> "Filter Cutoff" (8, default 1.0). 20*1000^v Hz (v=1->20kHz).
- tsFilterResonance -> "Filter Resonance" (9, default 0.0). Q.
- tsFilterEnvAmount -> "Filter Env Amount" (10, default 0.5). bipolar: v*2-1.
- tsDriveAmount -> "Drive Amount" (11, default 0.0). Saturator drive (now unity-makeup-compensated per M-2).
- tsFoldAmount -> "Fold Amount" (12, default 0.0). Wavefolder.
- tsPitchEnvStart -> "PitchEnv Start" (13, default 0.0). 20*100^v Hz (toLogNorm(hz)=ln(hz/20)/ln(100)). v=0->20Hz, v=1->2000Hz.
- tsPitchEnvEnd -> "PitchEnv End" (14, default 0.0). same 20*100^v Hz.
- tsPitchEnvTime -> "PitchEnv Time" (15, default 0.0). v*500 ms (v=0.1->50ms).
- tsPitchEnvCurve -> "PitchEnv Curve" (16, default 0.5). curveAmount = 2v-1 (0.5->0 linear; 0.15->-0.7 log/exp drop; segment-1 curve).
- tsFilterEnvAttack -> "Filter Env Atk" (17, default 0.0). CUBIC: v^3*500 ms.
- tsFilterEnvDecay -> "Filter Env Dec" (18, default 0.1). CUBIC: v^3*2000 ms (0.385->~114ms).
- tsFilterEnvSustain -> "Filter Env Sus" (19, default 0.0). [0,1].
- tsFilterEnvRelease -> "Filter Env Rel" (20, default 0.1). CUBIC: v^3*2000 ms.

UNNATURAL ZONE:
- modeStretch -> "Mode Stretch" (21, default 0.333333). denorm 0.5+v*1.5 (0.333->1.0 = physical/no stretch). Drives ModalResonatorBank inharmonicity B (B_max now 0.01 post-fix).
- decaySkew -> "Decay Skew" (22, default 0.5). bipolar v*2-1 (0.5->0). Per-mode amplitude tilt ratio^(-skew) (now on ALL bodies post-M-5).
- modeInjectAmount -> "Mode Inject" (23, default 0.0). Injects extra partials at 1/k slope (post-fix). Only experimentalKit uses (0.2).
- nonlinearCoupling -> "Nonlinear Coupling" (24, default 0.0). Now env-LEVEL-driven waveshaping (drive=1+kDriveScale*vel*amount*env) per M-3/M-4 redesign. amount=0 = exact bypass.

MATERIAL MORPH:
- morphEnabled -> "Morph Enabled" (25, bool >=0.5).
- morphStart -> "Morph Start" (26, default 1.0). material value at start.
- morphEnd -> "Morph End" (27, default 0.0). material value at end.
- morphDuration -> "Morph Duration" (28, default 0.095477). 10+v*1990 ms (0.0955->200ms).
- morphCurve -> "Morph Curve" (29, bool >=0.5: 0=Lin,1=Exp).

ROUTING (int):
- chokeGroup -> "Choke Group" (30). [0,8], 0=no choke.
- outputBus -> "Output Bus" (31). [0,15], 0=main.

EXCITER SECONDARY:
- fmRatio -> "FM Ratio" (32, default 0.133333). norm of [1,4]; default->1.4. FMImpulse modulator ratio.
- feedbackAmount -> "Feedback Amount" (33, default 0.0). Feedback exciter / FM feedback.
- noiseBurstDuration -> "NoiseBurst Duration" (34, default 0.5). Burst contact ms; generator often uses (ms-2)/13 mapping (~2-15ms).
- frictionPressure -> "Friction Pressure" (35, default 0.0). STK bow pressure (slope=5-4*pressure).

COUPLING/MACROS:
- couplingAmount -> "Coupling Amount" (36, default 0.5). Per-pad participation in global sympathetic coupling.
- macroTightness -> "Tightness" (37, default 0.5 neutral). MacroMapper delta.
- macroBrightness -> "Brightness" (38, default 0.5).
- macroBodySize -> "Body Size" (39, default 0.5).
- macroPunch -> "Punch" (40, default 0.5).
- macroComplexity -> "Complexity" (41, default 0.5).

NOISE LAYER (Phase 7):
- noiseLayerMix -> "Noise Mix" (42, default 0.35).
- noiseLayerCutoff -> "Noise Cutoff" (43, default 0.5).
- noiseLayerResonance -> "Noise Resonance" (44, default 0.2).
- noiseLayerDecay -> "Noise Decay" (45, default 0.3).
- noiseLayerColor -> "Noise Color" (46, default 0.5).

CLICK LAYER (Phase 7):
- clickLayerMix -> "Click Mix" (47, default 0.5).
- clickLayerContactMs -> "Click Contact" (48, default 0.3). norm: 0=2ms,1=5ms.
- clickLayerBrightness -> "Click Brightness" (49, default 0.6).

DAMPING (Phase 8A):
- bodyDampingB1 -> "Body Damping b1" (50, generator default 0.40; PadConfig sentinel -1.0='derive'). 0->0.2 s^-1, 1->50 s^-1.
- bodyDampingB3 -> "Body Damping b3" (51, generator default 0.40; sentinel -1.0). 0->0 (metallic), 1->8e-5 (woody HF damping).

AIR/SCATTER (Phase 8C):
- airLoading -> "Air Loading" (52, default 0.6). 0=pure Bessel, 1=full Rossing timpani-series depression.
- modeScatter -> "Mode Scatter" (53, default 0.0). 0=pure ratios, 1=15% sinusoidal dither.

COUPLING/SECONDARY (Phase 8D):
- couplingStrength -> "Coupling Strength" (54, default 0.0). Head<->shell feedforward drive (full 0..1 range post-L-7).
- secondaryEnabled -> "Secondary Enabled" (55, bool >=0.5).
- secondarySize -> "Secondary Size" (56, default 0.5). Shell f0 offset from head.
- secondaryMaterial -> "Secondary Material" (57, default 0.4).

TENSION (Phase 8E):
- tensionModAmt -> "Tension Mod" (58, default 0.0). Energy-dependent pitch glide (~2 semitones at full vel post-N-1 recalibration; reads getModalEnergy()).

ENABLE (Phase 8F):
- enabled -> "Pad Enabled" (59, bool >=0.5, default 1.0). disableUncraftedPads() sets 0 on non-crafted pads.

PITCH ENV KNEE (Phase 10):
- tsPitchEnvKneeEnabled -> "PitchEnv Knee" (60, bool, default 0.0/OFF).
- tsPitchEnvMidPitch -> "PitchEnv Mid" (61, default 0.5). 20*100^v Hz.
- tsPitchEnvMidFraction -> "PitchEnv Mid Frac" (62, default 0.5). fraction of total time.
- tsPitchEnvCurve2 -> "PitchEnv Curve2" (63, default 0.5). segment-2 curveAmount = 2v-1.

PAN (M-9):
- pan -> "Pan" (64=kPadPan, default 0.5=center). Equal-power: gainL=sqrt2*cos(pan*pi/2), gainR=sqrt2*sin(pan*pi/2). NOT a Pad-struct field that any kit sets - left default 0.5 in ALL 20 kits (stereo image unused).

WIRE NOTE: writePadToBuffer emits 57 float64 in PadSnapshot::sound order (NOT enum order): indices [0..27] core/toneshaper/unnatural/morph block, [28..29] choke/bus mirrors, [30..33] fm/feedback/noiseburst/friction, [34..38] noise layer, [39..41] click, [42..43] damping b1/b3, [44..45] airLoading/scatter, [46..49] coupling/secondaryEnabled/secondarySize/secondaryMaterial, [50] tension, [51] enabled, [52..55] pitchEnvKnee/mid/midFraction/curve2, [56] pan. Per-pad couplingAmount + 5 macros are written SEPARATELY (pad-major blocks after the per-pad loop), NOT in the 57-slot sound array. chokeGroup/outputBus also written as uint8 after the sound array.
